audit_items.proof_trace: The sole kzalloc (line 143) is unreachable on all failure paths before it (each returns NULL without allocating), and if kzalloc itself fails the NULL is propagated. After a successful allocation the remaining code is five plain field assignments with no error paths, so the pointer is always returned to the caller and freed via nfp_cpp_mutex_free/kfree — no internal leak is possible.
